Sample.Title: sc_A1: BY4741, WT, 2-hour

Channel.Growth-Protocol: BY4742 WT were grown on SD solid agar plates.
Channel.Extract-Protocol: Yeast cells were lysed with ~4µl lysis buffer, containing 0.5%Triton, 2.5µM oligo-dT, 2.5mM dNTP (Invitrogen,1959189), 8000 molecules of external RNA control consortium (ERCC) spike in, 3 x 10-2U/µl zymolyase(ZYMO, E1004-A), 1U/µl Recombinant RNase Inhibitor(TaKaRa, AI41189A), and RNA released. RNA libraries for RNA-seq were prepared using Smart-seq2 following manufacturer's protocols.

Sample.Data-Processing: Paired-end reads were mapped to the S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64 version (www.yeastgenome.org) with ERCC spike-in sequences added using HISAT2 (version 2.1.0). Resulting bam files were sorted and indexed using samtools (version 1.1). Final read counts mapped to genes were extracted using FeatureCounts. Assembly: S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64. Supplementary files format and content: tab-delimited text files include log2-normalized read counts for each Sample

Channel.Extract-Protocol: Yeast cells were lysed with ~4µl lysis buffer, containing 0.5%Triton, 2.5µM oligo-dT, 2.5mM dNTP (Invitrogen,1959189), 8000 molecules of external RNA control consortium (ERCC) spike in, 3 x 10-2U/µl zymolyase(ZYMO, E1004-A), 1U/µl Recombinant RNase Inhibitor(TaKaRa, AI41189A), and RNA released. RNA libraries for RNA-seq were prepared using Smart-seq2 following manufacturer's protocols.

Sample.Data-Processing: Paired-end reads were mapped to the S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64 version (www.yeastgenome.org) with ERCC spike-in sequences added using HISAT2 (version 2.1.0). Resulting bam files were sorted and indexed using samtools (version 1.1). Final read counts mapped to genes were extracted using FeatureCounts. Assembly: S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64. Supplementary files format and content: tab-delimited text files include log2-normalized read counts for each Sample

Channel.Extract-Protocol: Yeast cells were lysed with ~4µl lysis buffer, containing 0.5%Triton, 2.5µM oligo-dT, 2.5mM dNTP (Invitrogen,1959189), 8000 molecules of external RNA control consortium (ERCC) spike in, 3 x 10-2U/µl zymolyase(ZYMO, E1004-A), 1U/µl Recombinant RNase Inhibitor(TaKaRa, AI41189A), and RNA released. RNA libraries for RNA-seq were prepared using Smart-seq2 following manufacturer's protocols.

Sample.Data-Processing: Paired-end reads were mapped to the S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64 version (www.yeastgenome.org) with ERCC spike-in sequences added using HISAT2 (version 2.1.0). Resulting bam files were sorted and indexed using samtools (version 1.1). Final read counts mapped to genes were extracted using FeatureCounts. Assembly: S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64. Supplementary files format and content: tab-delimited text files include log2-normalized read counts for each Sample

Resulting bam files were sorted and indexed using samtools (version 1.1). Final read counts mapped to genes were extracted using FeatureCounts. Assembly: S288c Saccharomyces cerevisiae genome R64. Supplementary files format and content: tab-delimited text files include log2-normalized read counts for each Sample

Sample.Title: sc_C47: BY4741, WT, 36-hour

Channel.Extract-Protocol: Yeast cells were lysed with ~4µl lysis buffer, containing 0.5%Triton, 2.5µM oligo-dT, 2.5mM dNTP (Invitrogen,1959189), 8000 molecules of external RNA control consortium (ERCC) spike in, 3 x 10-2U/µl zymolyase(ZYMO, E1004-A), 1U/µl Recombinant RNase Inhibitor(TaKaRa, AI41189A), and RNA released. Series.Title: Single-cell RNA-seq reveals early heterogeneity during aging in yeast
Series.Summary: we carried out a systematic and quantitative investigation of yeast aging with single-cell resolution through transcriptomic sequencing. We optimized a single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) protocol to quantitatively study the whole transcriptome profiles of single yeast cells at different ages, finding increased cell-to-cell transcriptional variability during aging. The single-cell transcriptome analysis also highlighted key biological processes or cellular components, including oxidation-reduction process, oxidative stress response (OSR), translation, ribosome biogenesis and mitochondrion that underlie aging in yeast. We uncovered a molecular marker of FIT3, indicating the early heterogeneity during aging in yeast. We also analyzed the regulation of transcription factors and further characterized the distinctive temporal regulation of the OSR by YAP1 and proteasome activity by RPN4 during aging in yeast. Overall, our data profoundly reveal early heterogeneity during aging in yeast and shed light on the aging dynamics at the single cell level.
Series.Overall-Design: We selected relatively young and small sized cells from the yeast colonies and aligned them on the same agar plate and dissected daughter cells per 1.5 hours. After 2/16/36 hours, we isolated yeast mother cell and conducted scRNA-seq process. The data analysis reveal transcriptional change during aging.
Series.Type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
